currencyConverter.title: Currency Purchasing Power Converter
currencyConverter.description: Travel through time, understand the real value of ancient currency
currencyConverter.customPriceHint: Used to estimate current purchasable quantity
currencyConverter.riceHint: A modern person traveling to this dynasty with {{amount}} guan can buy {{rice}} jin of rice.
currencyConverter.flourHint: You can buy {{flour}} jin of flour, enough to make {{num}} steamed buns.
currencyConverter.clothHint: You can buy {{cloth}} bolts of cloth, enough to make {{num}} garments.
currencyConverter.meatHint: You can buy {{meat}} jin of pork, enough for {{num}} braised pork feasts.
currencyConverter.wineHint: You can buy {{wine}} jin of wine, enough for {{num}} guests.
currencyConverter.customHint: (Converted based on this dynasty’s rice price)
currencyConverter.priceNote: Note: Prices are based on historical records; actual prices vary by region and season.
currencyConverter.guanDesc: Guan is the most common ancient currency unit. 1 Guan = 1000 wen of copper coins. In the Song dynasty and later, paper money (jiaozi, huizi) and silver became main currencies, but  guan was still widely used.
currencyConverter.wenDesc: Wen is the smallest currency unit, 1 wen = 1 copper coin. In ancient times, one wen could buy 1 sheng of rice (about 1 jin).
currencyConverter.liangDesc: Liang is a weight unit, became a main currency unit in the Song dynasty and later. 1 liang = 10 qian = 100 fen. One liang of silver is approximately equivalent to 1 guan of copper coins or 1000 wen.
currencyConverter.goldSilverDesc: Gold-silver ratios varied by dynasty. Generally: Tang 1 liang gold = 4 liang silver; Song 1 liang gold = 2 liang silver; Yuan 1 liang gold = 1 liang silver (gold became a main currency); Ming-Qing 1 liang gold = 0.5~0.8 liang silver.

aiHistorian.style_eulogy_desc: Posthumous title appraisal format, formal and solemn
aiHistorian.chat_subtitle: The historian remembers your portrait and previous commentary, feel free to follow up
aiHistorian.chat_hint: Commentary is ready. You may now ask the historian any historical question. The historian remembers your portrait and commentary to stay consistent.
aiHistorian.q_mirror_figure: Why is {{name}} said to be my mirror?
aiHistorian.q_dynasty_details: I am interested in {{dynasty}}. What underrated details does this dynasty have?
aiHistorian.q_learn_one_thing: If I could learn only one thing from {{name}}, what should it be?
aiHistorian.q_historian_judgment: How would historians judge this kind of "portrait" of me?

ragQa.title: History Wisdom Q&A
ragQa.description: Answering history questions based on historical sources
ragQa.input_placeholder: Ask any history question… e.g., How exactly did the An Lushan Rebellion affect the Tang economy?
ragQa.request_failed: Request failed, please check API Key configuration
ragQa.answer_subtitle: Based on historical source retrieval, not fabricated
ragQa.suggestion_1: How exactly did the An Lushan Rebellion affect the Tang economy?
ragQa.suggestion_2: What strategy did Qin Shi Huang use to unify the six states?
ragQa.suggestion_3: Why did the Song Dynasty emphasize civil over military?
ragQa.suggestion_4: Was the fall of the Ming Dynasty related to climate?
ragQa.suggestion_5: What disagreements did Confucius and Laozi have?
ragQa.suggestion_6: Which was more prosperous, the Tang or the Song?
ragQa.suggestion_7: Why did Cao Cao lose the Battle of Red Cliffs?
ragQa.suggestion_8: How did the Four Great Inventions reach the West?

calendarConverter.title: Historical Calendar Converter
calendarConverter.subtitle: Enter a Gregorian year → Ganzhi year + Chinese Zodiac + Reign Period
calendarConverter.no_reign_data: No reign period data for this year
calendarConverter.tip_ganzhi: Ganzhi Year: Combines Heavenly Stems (Jia Yi Bing Ding Wu Ji Geng Xin Ren Gui) with Earthly Branches (Zi Chou Yin Mao Chen Si Wu Wei Shen You Xu Hai), forming a 60-year cycle
calendarConverter.tip_reign: Reign Period: Named by emperors; one emperor may have multiple reign periods (e.g., Emperor Wu of Han had 11), but since the Ming dynasty each emperor typically uses one
calendarConverter.tip_ad: AD/BC Dating: Based on the birth of Jesus; not used in Chinese history historically, introduced in modern times
calendarConverter.tip_key_years: Key Years: 221 BC = 26th year of Qin Shi Huang (Qin unification); AD 755 = 14th year of Tianbao, Tang (An Lushan Rebellion)

contribution.title_required: Title and content cannot be empty

jobs.label: JOBS
jobs.title: Ancient Occupations
jobs.description: Travel through time to learn about ancient people's lives and jobs
jobs.advice_common: This is the most common job, suitable for beginners. Requires basic physical or skill, relatively stable life.
jobs.advice_rare: This is a relatively rare job requiring special talents or resources. Less competition but more challenges.
jobs.advice_elite: This is an elite job requiring top professional skills. High income but high pressure, requires perfect performance.
jobs.advice_royal: This is a royal job, hard to reach for ordinary people. Requires extremely high status or special opportunities.

plagueHistory.label: PLAGUE
plagueHistory.title: Historical Plagues and Civilization
plagueHistory.description: Learn about major epidemics in Chinese history and their impact on civilization
plagueHistory.lesson_1: 1. Epidemics are closely related to social turmoil; famine and war exacerbate outbreaks
plagueHistory.lesson_2: 2. Traditional Chinese medicine played an important role in epidemic prevention, but modern medicine is more effective
plagueHistory.lesson_3: 3. Public health systems are key to epidemic control
plagueHistory.lesson_4: 4. Early isolation and preventive measures can significantly reduce mortality

historicalTrial.label: TRIAL
historicalTrial.title: Historical Tribunal
historicalTrial.description: Travel back to ancient times and witness historic trials
historicalTrial.analyze_intro: AI will analyze this case from both modern legal and historical context dimensions.
historicalTrial.judge_intro: Before viewing the historical verdict, make your own judgment, then AI will compare yours with history.
historicalTrial.learn_intro: Legal knowledge points derived from this case, helping you understand the differences between ancient and modern legal concepts.

imperialExamination.label: EXAMINATION
imperialExamination.title: Imperial Examination Simulator
imperialExamination.description: Travel back to ancient times and experience the imperial examination
imperialExamination.answer_match_label: Match left items with right items:
imperialExamination.knowledge_1: 1. The imperial exam had four stages: county, provincial, metropolitan, and palace examination
imperialExamination.knowledge_2: 2. Ming-Qing exams used Four Books and Five Classics as content, with Cheng-Zhu Neo-Confucianism as the main ideology
imperialExamination.knowledge_3: 3. Essays were required to strictly follow the eight-legged essay format
imperialExamination.knowledge_4: 4. The palace exam was presided over by the emperor himself, determining final rankings

architectureMortise.feature_3_desc: Mortise-tenon joints make the building integral, every member participates in load-bearing
architectureMortise.feature_4_desc: Mortise-tenon structures can be easily disassembled and reassembled for repair
